commands.review_summary:
This PR extracts status-item icon observation into a new extension, narrows the signature to rendered icon state, adds a cheap Codex credits helper, and expands signature tests.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was run in this read-only review. Source inspection does confirm current main still observes all provider snapshots via String(describing: snapshot), which matches the reported hot path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-provided real behavior proof and maintainer review of a UI caching boundary, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: Reduce merged icon observation churn
target.body:
Refs #1274.

## Summary
- Move the menu bar icon observation signature out of the broad status item controller file.
- Remove full `UsageSnapshot` stringification from the icon observation signature.
- In Merge Icons mode, sign only the provider actually rendered by the merged icon plus aggregate provider status, instead of every provider snapshot.
- Replace the Codex menu-bar credit projection in the icon render/observation path with a cheap credits/rate-window helper.

## Why
`@giuseppebisemi` sampled the residual lag after #1286 and the hot path was `observeStoreIconChanges -> storeIconObservationSignature -> providerStoreIconObservationSignature`. The old signature subscribed to every provider snapshot and used `String(describing: snapshot)`, so account/email/timestamp and non-rendered provider churn could retrigger icon work even when the visible merged icon would not change.

## Validation
- `swift build`
- `swift build --build-tests`
- `xcrun xctest -XCTest CodexBarTests.StatusItemIconObservationSignatureTests .build/arm64-apple-macosx/debug/CodexBarPackageTests.xctest` (8 Swift Testing tests passed)
- `make check`
target.author: hhh2210
